name: PyPortfolioOpt (Portfolio Optimization)

performance.notes: scipy/cvxpy optimization; fast for typical portfolio sizes (<500 assets)

agent_readiness.notes: Clean portfolio optimization API. from pypfopt import EfficientFrontier, risk_models, expected_returns. mu = expected_returns.mean_historical_return(prices). S = risk_models.sample_cov(prices). ef = EfficientFrontier(mu, S). weights = ef.max_sharpe(). clean_weights = ef.clean_weights(). Discrete allocation: DiscreteAllocation(weights, prices, total_portfolio_value=10000). Hierarchical Risk Parity: HRPOpt. Black-Litterman: BlackLittermanModel. Cleaner API than Riskfolio-Lib; fewer risk measures but easier to use.

gotchas: Limited risk measures vs Riskfolio-Lib (no CVaR optimization in base)
Covariance estimation sensitive to lookback period
Discrete allocation rounding can deviate from target weights
Expected returns estimation is notoriously difficult in practice
best_when: Mean-variance optimization with clean Pythonic API
Discrete share allocation for equity portfolios
Hierarchical Risk Parity (HRP) as alternative to MVO
Teaching portfolio theory with readable code
summary: PyPortfolioOpt provides clean portfolio optimization: EfficientFrontier(mu, S).max_sharpe() for mean-variance, HRPOpt for hierarchical risk parity, BlackLittermanModel for views-based allocation. DiscreteAllocation for share count rounding. Better API ergonomics than Riskfolio-Lib. Use for standard MVO, HRP, Black-Litterman. Use Riskfolio-Lib for advanced CVaR/CDaR risk measures.
